report.name: Operations Assessment Waste Walk - Distribution Center

categories.name: Production

attachments.name: waste_walk_warehouse_observations.jpg
attachments.description: Waste walk findings showing mixed performance with some good practices but significant waste opportunities

categories.description: Warehouse operations show mixed waste elimination performance with some effective practices offset by significant improvement opportunities.

statements.name: Waiting waste visible at receiving dock with trucks queuing
statements.value: Average 45-minute wait times for unloading with no visual scheduling system

statements.name: Motion waste evident with excessive walking between pick locations
statements.value: Pickers walking average 12 miles per shift due to random storage locations

statements.name: Some pull signals in place but inconsistent implementation
statements.value: Kanban cards used in 30% of areas but often ignored or missing

statements.name: Basic standard work exists but compliance varies significantly
statements.value: Written procedures available but different methods observed between shifts

categories.name: Quality
categories.description: Quality systems showing basic implementation with reactive approach and limited waste prevention measures.

statements.name: Defect waste from picking errors averaging 2.5% error rate
statements.value: Manual picking process with limited verification causing customer complaints

statements.name: Rework areas indicate acceptance of defects rather than prevention
statements.value: Dedicated rework station processing 50+ items daily suggesting systemic issues

statements.name: Limited error-proofing with mostly manual processes
statements.value: Barcode scanning available but not consistently used for verification

statements.name: Skills underutilized with no formal improvement process
statements.value: Employees have ideas but no mechanism to capture or implement suggestions

categories.name: Maintenance

attachments.name: waste_walk_equipment_issues.jpg
attachments.description: Equipment-related waste findings including breakdowns and inefficient operations

categories.description: Maintenance practices primarily reactive leading to equipment-related wastes and operational disruptions.

statements.name: Equipment breakdowns cause waiting waste averaging 2 hours daily
statements.value: Forklift failures and conveyor stoppages with no preventive maintenance

statements.name: Motion waste as maintenance searches for tools and parts
statements.value: No organized tool storage resulting in 30+ minutes searching per repair

statements.name: Overburden on equipment operating beyond rated capacity
statements.value: Forklifts consistently carrying 120% of rated load to meet demands

statements.name: Environmental waste from inefficient equipment operation
statements.value: Older equipment consuming 40% more energy than modern alternatives

categories.name: Administration
categories.description: Administrative processes showing significant overprocessing and inventory waste with manual systems.

statements.name: Paper-based systems create inventory waste of documents
statements.value: Multiple filing cabinets with duplicate records and outdated information

statements.name: Overprocessing in data entry with multiple system inputs
statements.value: Same information entered into 3-4 different systems manually

statements.name: Social responsibility waste with unclear technology policies
statements.value: Personal device use during work affecting productivity and safety

statements.name: Unevenness in administrative workload causing delays
statements.value: End-of-month bottlenecks with overtime while mid-month has idle time

highlightsData.title: Significant Waiting Waste
highlightsData.description:
Dock Congestion: Receiving dock experiences average 45-minute truck wait times with no appointment system or visual scheduling, creating cascading delays throughout operations.

Information Delays: Manual paperwork processing adds 20-30 minutes per shipment with frequent missing documentation requiring follow-up calls and emails.

highlightsData.title: Excessive Motion Patterns
highlightsData.description:
Inefficient Pick Paths: Random storage locations force pickers to traverse entire warehouse multiple times per order, walking average 12 miles per shift versus 4-mile benchmark.

Poor Ergonomics: High-frequency items stored on top shelves requiring ladder use while low shelves contain slow-moving inventory.

highlightsData.title: Transport Inefficiencies
highlightsData.description:
Multiple Handling: Products moved 4-5 times from receiving to shipping due to poor layout and temporary storage areas scattered throughout facility.

Cross-Traffic Patterns: Inbound and outbound flows intersect creating safety hazards and requiring additional transport time.

highlightsData.title: Administrative Overprocessing
highlightsData.description:
Redundant Data Entry: Same shipment information manually entered into WMS, TMS, billing system, and spreadsheets, consuming 2 hours daily per clerk.

Excessive Reporting: Multiple reports generated daily that no one reviews, with managers admitting they only use 20% of provided information.

highlightsData.title: Inventory Accumulation
highlightsData.description:
Excess Safety Stock: Fear of stockouts drives 3-month inventory levels for items with 2-week lead times, tying up capital and warehouse space unnecessarily.

Obsolete Inventory: 15% of warehouse space occupied by slow-moving or obsolete items with no disposition process established.

highlightsData.title: Quality Defects Impact
highlightsData.description:
Picking Errors: 2.5% error rate in order fulfillment requires dedicated rework station processing 50+ corrections daily, doubling handling costs for affected orders.

Damage During Handling: Improper handling techniques and equipment result in 1% damage rate with no root cause analysis conducted.

highlightsData.title: Underutilized Skills
highlightsData.description:
No Improvement Process: Employees report having improvement ideas but no formal suggestion system exists, with past informal suggestions ignored or forgotten.

Limited Development: Cross-training minimal with employees locked into single roles, creating bottlenecks when key personnel absent.

highlightsData.title: Workload Unevenness
highlightsData.description:
Demand Fluctuations: Monday/Friday volumes 200% of mid-week creating overtime needs and service failures while Wednesday sees idle time and make-work activities.

Shift Imbalances: Day shift handles 70% of volume while night shift underutilized, creating stress and quality issues during peak periods.

actionItems.title: Implement Dock Scheduling System
actionItems.description: Deploy appointment scheduling for inbound/outbound shipments to eliminate waiting waste and improve dock utilization

attachments.name: waste_reduction_priorities.jpg
attachments.description: Priority waste reduction opportunities identified during walk

actionItems.title: Redesign Warehouse Layout
actionItems.description: Conduct ABC analysis and redesign layout to minimize motion and transport waste with optimized pick paths

actionItems.title: Establish Error-Proofing Systems
actionItems.description: Implement barcode scanning verification at pick and pack stations to reduce defect waste

attachments.name: quality_improvement_plan.jpg
attachments.description: Quality system improvements to eliminate defect waste

actionItems.title: Create Employee Suggestion System
actionItems.description: Launch formal suggestion program with review process to capture improvement ideas and engage workforce
